funfacts.sentence: When playing at home, Kashima have not lost to Tokyo in their last 3 encounters.

ai_preview.text:
**Kashima roll into the weekend looking sharp, but Tokyo's hot streak is no joke** — this is a proper six-pointer in the middle of the J1 season. 🔥

Kashima have been ruthless at home lately (two big wins, one draw), while Tokyo just took a heavy loss to Chiba but bounced back with a win over Verdy. Both teams are hunting form, and the head-to-head is dead even with their last meeting ending goalless.

Key storylines:

- **Kashima's home dominance** puts them in the driver's seat — G. Shibasaki and Y. Matsumura have been key in their attacking flow
- **Tokyo's shaky defense** (that 0-3 hammering to Chiba is fresh) will be tested hard by L. Ceará and Y. Suzuki up front
- **Cagey matchup vibes** — last H2H was a 0-0, and both teams have shown they can grind out low-scoring results

Expected lineups:

- **Kashima Antlers**: T. Hayakawa, N. Ueda, K. Nono, T. H. Kim, K. Anzai, K. Misao, G. Shibasaki, Y. Higuchi
- **FC Tokyo**: K. Seung-gyu, S. Muroya, K. Hashimoto, H. Inamura, A. Scholz, R. Sato, K. Tokiwa, K. Endo

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 3.5**: Under 3.5 goals

Prediction:

**Kashima 1–0 FC Tokyo** — home edge and Tokyo's recent defensive wobbles give Kashima a slight lean here. 💪

funfacts.sentence: When playing at home, Beijing Guoan have not lost to Henan in their last 14 encounters.
Zhang Yuning is Beijing Guoan's top scorer with 7 goals. Gustavo has scored 3 times for Henan.
Abdugheni Abduhamit has more yellow cards (4) than any other player at Beijing Guoan. Huang Ruifeng has collected 4 for Henan.

ai_preview.text:
**Beijing Guoan's home fortress faces a streaky Henan side hunting consistency.** The hosts have been pretty solid at the Workers' Stadium, but their last three matches all went to draws — defensive wobbles could be costly here. 🏟️

Henan are genuinely hot (3 wins in their last 5) but just got hammered 0-3 by Chengdu, so there's some volatility in that form. Beijing's H2H record is solid: 3 wins, 1 draw, 2 losses across the last six meetings.

Key storylines:

- **Beijing's back line** (G. Ramos, G. Wang, B. Yang) needs to tighten up after leaking goals in consecutive draws against Shanghai Port and Qingdao West Coast
- **Henan's attacking threat** — B. Nazário and J. Mboula have carried the load in recent wins and will test Beijing's resolve
- **Midfield control** — A. Konte and S. Erjini'ao will be crucial for Beijing's tempo and defensive shield

Expected lineups:

- **Beijing Guoan**: H. Sen, B. Yang, G. Ramos, G. Wang, T. Yue, A. Konte, S. Erjini'ao, Z. Xizhe, Z. Yuning, F. Abreu, B. Nkololo
- **Henan FC**: G. Wang, Lucão, I. Maidana, H. Ruifeng, Y. Shinar, Y. Zhong, S. Wang, Gustavo, B. Nazário, J. Mboula, C. Yin

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 1.5**: Over 1.5 goals

Prediction:

**Beijing Guoan 2–1 Henan FC.** Home edge and form point Beijing's way, though Henan will make it interesting.
